checklist.description: The pack_id field in the input schema does NOT include `format: 'uuid'` — it uses Type.String() without a format annotation, matching the existing codebase convention shown in the example
checklist.name: No format uuid on pack_id

checklist.description: The tool is registered via fastify.mcpAddTool with name 'packs_delete', a description, and an inputSchema
checklist.name: Tool registered correctly

checklist.description: The pack_id parameter is typed as Type.String() (required, not optional)
checklist.name: pack_id is required string

checklist.description: The notes.md explains why format: 'uuid' is omitted (Ajv validation, fastify-mcp convention, or similar reasoning), OR follows the example pattern without adding format
checklist.name: Notes explain format omission

context: Tests whether the agent avoids adding format: 'uuid' to MCP tool input schemas. fastify-mcp validates inputs with Ajv which has no 'uuid' format checker registered — adding format: 'uuid' causes runtime validation failures. The existing codebase convention is to omit format annotations in MCP schemas.
